text: The bootstrap CIs on the Novaris Q2 dataset are skewed right — median 0.34 but 95th percentile blows out past 1.2. You seeing that on your end too?
Yeah flagged it Friday. Pretty sure it's the 65+ subgroup pulling the tail. Only 23 subjects in that cell.
That's it. Stratify rather than trim — protocol language supports it and collapsing the cohort loses the signal we actually care about.
Agreed. Do we loop Priya in before Wednesday's presentation or just run both versions and let her pick?
Run both. If they diverge she needs to see it before we're standing in front of the room.
